activities_projects.title: Observing Binary Fission in Amoeba
activities_projects.objective: Identify the process of asexual reproduction in single-celled organisms
materials: Permanent slides of Amoeba showing binary fission
steps: Place the permanent slide under the microscope.
Observe the nucleus lengthening and the body of the Amoeba constricting in the middle.
Look for slides showing the final stage where two daughter cells are formed.
Draw the stages in sequence in your notebook.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that binary fission results in the division of a parent cell into two identical individuals.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequential drawing of the stages of fission (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Budding in Yeast
activities_projects.objective: Observe how small outgrowths lead to new individuals
steps: Mix a pinch of yeast in a warm sugar solution and leave it for an hour.
Take a drop of this culture on a slide and cover it with a cover slip.
Observe under the microscope for small 'buds' sticking to the parent yeast cells.
Identify chains of buds still attached to the parent cell.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands budding as a method where a new organism grows as an outgrowth from the parent.
activities_projects.evaluation: Labelled diagram of a yeast cell with a developing bud (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bread Mould Observation (Rhizopus)
activities_projects.objective: Observe spore formation as a method of reproduction
steps: Moisten a slice of bread and leave it in a cool, dark place inside a box for 2-3 days.
Observe the thread-like white structures (hyphae) and black blobs (sporangia) appearing on it.
Examine the black blobs under a magnifying glass.
Discuss how these 'blobs' contain tiny seeds called spores.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies spore formation as a survival and reproductive strategy in fungi.
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the appearance of sporangia and their role in reproduction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Regeneration in Planaria (Conceptual)
activities_projects.objective: Understand how organisms can regrow from body parts
steps: Study the diagram of Planaria being cut into three pieces (head, middle, tail).
Draw the outcome where each piece grows the missing parts to become a full worm.
Research why complex animals like humans cannot regrow a whole body from a finger.
Define 'Regeneration' in your own words.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between simple fragmentation/regeneration and complex reproduction.
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why regeneration is not the same as 'planned' reproduction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vegetative Propagation in Potato
activities_projects.objective: Observe how plants reproduce without seeds
steps: Identify the 'eyes' on a potato.
Cut the potato into pieces, ensuring each piece has at least one 'eye'.
Bury these pieces in moist soil.
Observe the growth of new green shoots over the next week.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that certain plant parts like tubers can produce new plants under right conditions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Recording the number of days taken for the first shoot to appear (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Leaf Margins of Bryophyllum
steps: Observe the notches along the margin of a Bryophyllum leaf.
Place the leaf on moist soil and wait for 4-5 days.
Observe tiny plantlets with roots developing from the notches.
Explain how these plantlets can grow into independent plants.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that leaves can also serve as organs for vegetative propagation.
activities_projects.evaluation: Sketch of a Bryophyllum leaf showing plantlets at the margins (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Dissecting a Hibiscus Flower
activities_projects.objective: Identify the reproductive parts of a flower
steps: Identify the green sepals and colored petals.
Carefully remove the petals to see the stamens (male part).
Identify the anther and filament.
Look for the central pistil (female part) and identify the stigma, style, and ovary.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between the male (stamen) and female (pistil) reproductive organs in a plant.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of at least four floral parts on a diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Seed Germination Study
activities_projects.objective: Observe the growth of a seed into a seedling
steps: Soak a few seeds overnight in water.
Place them on wet cotton wool in a petri dish.
Observe daily and identify the Radicle (future root) and Plumule (future shoot).
Split a soaked seed to see the stored food in the Cotyledons.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that a seed contains a baby plant (embryo) and stored food for its growth.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of plumule and radicle in a germinating seed (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Reproductive System Chart
activities_projects.objective: Locate the primary organs of the human reproductive system
materials: Blank diagrams of male and female reproductive systems
steps: Label the Testes, Vas deferens, and Penis in the male system.
Label the Ovaries, Fallopian tubes (Oviducts), and Uterus in the female system.
Mark the site where fertilization takes place (Oviduct).
Identify where the embryo develops (Uterus).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Associates specific organs with their primary reproductive functions in humans.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct placement of labels for the primary reproductive organs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Puberty Changes Checklist
steps: Read about the common changes in both boys and girls during puberty (e.g., hair growth, voice changes).
List three changes that are common to both.
List two changes specific to boys and two specific to girls.
Explain why these changes are necessary for reaching reproductive maturity.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes puberty as the period of physical and hormonal changes leading to sexual maturity.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of common and gender-specific puberty changes (10 marks)
